title: Creator economy platform Patreon cuts 20% of staff in new layoffs
excerpt: Patreon, which helps creators like YouTubers and podcasters make money, is laying off staff. The creator economy company said it needs to trim costs.
content_html:
Patreon is laying off 20% of its staff. Read the memo its CEO sent to employees.

By Sydney Bradley

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

Patreon, a platform for creators that paywalls their content, is laying off staff.

In a publicly shared note addressed to Patreon's content creators, CEO Jack Conte announced that the creator-economy startup is laying off 93 employees, about 20% of its staff.

Conte said in a separate memo to staffers that the "market in which we operate has undergone profound change over the last 6 months," and that the company needs to cut costs to "remain a stable, dependable rock for our creators."

And yes, AI does play a role in it, Conte said.

"AI has fundamentally transformed the tech industry, the pace of change has never been more intense, and I expect it to get even faster," Conte said in the memo.

However, Patreon emphasized that these layoffs were not because the company thinks AI can replace humans or creativity.

In March, Conte told Business Insider that while AI can help people create "beautiful things," companies shouldn't roll out AI tools "in a way that just creates a bloodbath for the world's creative people."

Conte also published a video about the topic, recommending that AI companies start paying creators.

Patreon's ethos as a company is based on paying creators. Founded in 2013, it's become a popular platform for creators — like YouTubers and podcasters — to have fans pay for content and community.

The company became a unicorn in 2020 as the creator economy picked up steam, and was valued at $4 billion in 2021. Like much of the broader tech and media industries, Patreon has weathered financial headwinds since. In September 2022, the company laid off 80 people, about 17% of its staff at the time.

Patreon has faced many new competitors in the space, like Substack, Beehiiv, YouTube's own membership tiers, and white-label apps made by creators.

It's not the only company in the creator economy cutting staff. In February, creator-commerce platform LTK shed staff, and other tech companies, including Meta, Snapchat, and LinkedIn, have also laid off workers this year.

Following this recent round of layoffs, Conte said in the memo to staffers that the company would make changes to its "organizational structure and how we work" — namely, by "flattening" the company's teams.

The company will continue to focus on its products for creators.

"Patreon's core business is healthy and strong, and we're going to be a rock for creators for decades to come," Conte said in the post shared with creators. "We're not changing our roadmap or priorities. Patreon will continue shipping new features, improvements to our core experiences, media and community products, and more."

Read the full memo Patreon sent to its staff:

Hi Team,
I have a painful update this morning. I have decided to reduce the size of our team by 20%, or 93 people. All teammates at the company will receive an email within the next few minutes indicating whether or not your role is impacted by this change.
I'll get into the reasons for this decision and logistics for the day below, but before I do, I want to acknowledge how painful this will be for the kind, ambitious, hard working, and mission-driven humans who make up this company. The people at Patreon are the core of our spirit and what makes our culture and business so special. Today will be hard for so many people, and as the person responsible for this decision and the decisions leading up to this, I don't take that lightly. Please take the time you need today to support our departing teammates and yourself.
What's changing and why
There are two key changes we are making today:
  1. A workforce reduction
  2. Changing our organizational structure and how we work
I'll explain each below.
1. The workforce reduction
Nothing about our core business has changed — it remains strong and consistent:
  • More than 300,000 creators in nearly every country in the world are earning money on our platform.
  • Creators are earning billions of dollars each year on Patreon, and those earnings have continued to exhibit steady, strong growth.
  • Creators, members, revenue, and processing volume continue to show strong, consistent growth every month.
On top of that, our transformation to becoming a media and community network is starting to work:
  • Our network is now sending 1.5M new members to creators every month.
  • Creators have added ~200M free memberships over the past 3 years.
  • Feed-attributed memberships are up more than 5X since our network launch.
But our transformation is going to take time. We are setting our sights on massive problems: the attention economy is optimizing for addiction instead of long term relationships. Creators are finding it increasingly difficult to reach their followers and build strong communities. The legacy social platforms are locking creators into their systems instead of giving them independence. There is no company better positioned than Patreon to tackle these problems.
At the same time, the market in which we operate has undergone profound change over the last 6 months. We need to adjust our cost structure to ensure that we remain a stable, dependable rock for our creators as we work toward our long term ambition. The changes we're making today are painful, but they're necessary to ensure that Patreon can take the time we need to address the above problems from a position of strength.
2. Changing our organizational structure and how we work
We're making a number of changes to our organizational structure and how we work. Specifically, we're flattening the organization, refocusing teams on our top priorities, and evolving key aspects of our operations to make us faster at adapting to change.
We're making those changes because with a smaller team, we need a new organizational structure to continue executing effectively on our strategy and priorities, which are not changing. We remain focused on:
  • Improving core creator and fan experiences on the platform, and
  • Helping creators grow their audiences and businesses through our network
In addition, I want us to have increased agility as a company. AI has fundamentally transformed the tech industry, the pace of change has never been more intense, and I expect it to get even faster. Each of us will play a role in redefining how we work together as the nature of our work continues to change over time.
AI is not a replacement for human creativity
One of the most discussed questions over the last few months has been whether AI is replacing humans.
To be clear about the impact of AI on today's decision: we are not making the above changes because we believe AI replaces humans.
The more we have learned to use these new tools, the clearer it has become that they are not substitutes for the creativity, judgment, detail orientation, or craftsmanship that our teammates have in spades, nor do they replace the desire for human connection that all of us cherish so deeply. That's my personal opinion, but more importantly, it's the foundation of Patreon's strategy: our product vision and business are both predicated on the value of human creativity and human connection.
AI has fundamentally transformed the tech industry, though, including how we work, how we build products, how we communicate, and more. That does have an impact on how we operate and organize.
Logistics for Today
To our teammates leaving:
In the email you receive from our People team, you'll find details about next steps, severance packages, and information about how to connect with a member of the People team if you have any questions. You'll also receive a separate message from a member of our senior leadership team inviting you to a personal call if you would like.
You'll have access to your Patreon laptop and Slack through 5PM PT today. Access to all other internal systems will be removed, because we want to ensure we protect our creators and their businesses.
I know how important it is for folks to be able to say goodbye, so we've created a #gratitude-and-farewell Slack channel as a place for all of us to connect, if you feel comfortable.
Severance details:
We've prepared a severance package to support impacted employees through this transition:
  • 16 weeks of pay starting from today, including remaining on payroll through our August 20th vesting date.
  • One additional week of pay for every full year worked at the company.
  • Additional cash payment to accommodate a) recent hires who haven't reached their 1-year cliff, and b) more-tenured teammates who haven't yet received their 2026 refresh grant.
  • Healthcare coverage through the end of the year for eligible employees and families.
  • A $1,500 stipend to replace your company laptop.
Thank you for everything you've given our creators, fans, and this company. Your contributions have made a massive difference in the lives of so many people, and I'm deeply grateful for the energy, craft, and care you've put into your work. That work is now a part of Patreon and will continue to benefit creators and their communities for years to come.
To our teammates remaining:
The focus for today will be on our departing teammates, but we've scheduled a short video call for those of us remaining to be together as a team. Tomorrow, we'll have an all hands and Q&A to review the details of the reorganization and changes to how we work. Next week, we'll get together in person as planned to dive deeper on our H2 plans.
For over a decade, Patreon has been growing and evolving to ensure that creators are properly compensated for the value they create in the world. We have successfully adapted and thrived through recessions, the chaos of online media, and multiple market shifts. We continue to come out the other side stronger than before, and that's exactly what we'll do again.

blocks.html_en:
Hi Team,
I have a painful update this morning. I have decided to reduce the size of our team by 20%, or 93 people. All teammates at the company will receive an email within the next few minutes indicating whether or not your role is impacted by this change.
I'll get into the reasons for this decision and logistics for the day below, but before I do, I want to acknowledge how painful this will be for the kind, ambitious, hard working, and mission-driven humans who make up this company. The people at Patreon are the core of our spirit and what makes our culture and business so special. Today will be hard for so many people, and as the person responsible for this decision and the decisions leading up to this, I don't take that lightly. Please take the time you need today to support our departing teammates and yourself.
What's changing and why
There are two key changes we are making today:
  1. A workforce reduction
  2. Changing our organizational structure and how we work
I'll explain each below.
1. The workforce reduction
Nothing about our core business has changed — it remains strong and consistent:
  • More than 300,000 creators in nearly every country in the world are earning money on our platform.
  • Creators are earning billions of dollars each year on Patreon, and those earnings have continued to exhibit steady, strong growth.
  • Creators, members, revenue, and processing volume continue to show strong, consistent growth every month.
On top of that, our transformation to becoming a media and community network is starting to work:
  • Our network is now sending 1.5M new members to creators every month.
  • Creators have added ~200M free memberships over the past 3 years.
  • Feed-attributed memberships are up more than 5X since our network launch.
But our transformation is going to take time. We are setting our sights on massive problems: the attention economy is optimizing for addiction instead of long term relationships. Creators are finding it increasingly difficult to reach their followers and build strong communities. The legacy social platforms are locking creators into their systems instead of giving them independence. There is no company better positioned than Patreon to tackle these problems.
At the same time, the market in which we operate has undergone profound change over the last 6 months. We need to adjust our cost structure to ensure that we remain a stable, dependable rock for our creators as we work toward our long term ambition. The changes we're making today are painful, but they're necessary to ensure that Patreon can take the time we need to address the above problems from a position of strength.
2. Changing our organizational structure and how we work
We're making a number of changes to our organizational structure and how we work. Specifically, we're flattening the organization, refocusing teams on our top priorities, and evolving key aspects of our operations to make us faster at adapting to change.
We're making those changes because with a smaller team, we need a new organizational structure to continue executing effectively on our strategy and priorities, which are not changing. We remain focused on:
  • Improving core creator and fan experiences on the platform, and
  • Helping creators grow their audiences and businesses through our network
In addition, I want us to have increased agility as a company. AI has fundamentally transformed the tech industry, the pace of change has never been more intense, and I expect it to get even faster. Each of us will play a role in redefining how we work together as the nature of our work continues to change over time.
AI is not a replacement for human creativity
One of the most discussed questions over the last few months has been whether AI is replacing humans.
To be clear about the impact of AI on today's decision: we are not making the above changes because we believe AI replaces humans.
The more we have learned to use these new tools, the clearer it has become that they are not substitutes for the creativity, judgment, detail orientation, or craftsmanship that our teammates have in spades, nor do they replace the desire for human connection that all of us cherish so deeply. That's my personal opinion, but more importantly, it's the foundation of Patreon's strategy: our product vision and business are both predicated on the value of human creativity and human connection.
AI has fundamentally transformed the tech industry, though, including how we work, how we build products, how we communicate, and more. That does have an impact on how we operate and organize.
Logistics for Today
To our teammates leaving:
In the email you receive from our People team, you'll find details about next steps, severance packages, and information about how to connect with a member of the People team if you have any questions. You'll also receive a separate message from a member of our senior leadership team inviting you to a personal call if you would like.
You'll have access to your Patreon laptop and Slack through 5PM PT today. Access to all other internal systems will be removed, because we want to ensure we protect our creators and their businesses.
I know how important it is for folks to be able to say goodbye, so we've created a #gratitude-and-farewell Slack channel as a place for all of us to connect, if you feel comfortable.
Severance details:
We've prepared a severance package to support impacted employees through this transition:
  • 16 weeks of pay starting from today, including remaining on payroll through our August 20th vesting date.
  • One additional week of pay for every full year worked at the company.
  • Additional cash payment to accommodate a) recent hires who haven't reached their 1-year cliff, and b) more-tenured teammates who haven't yet received their 2026 refresh grant.
  • Healthcare coverage through the end of the year for eligible employees and families.
  • A $1,500 stipend to replace your company laptop.
Thank you for everything you've given our creators, fans, and this company. Your contributions have made a massive difference in the lives of so many people, and I'm deeply grateful for the energy, craft, and care you've put into your work. That work is now a part of Patreon and will continue to benefit creators and their communities for years to come.
To our teammates remaining:
The focus for today will be on our departing teammates, but we've scheduled a short video call for those of us remaining to be together as a team. Tomorrow, we'll have an all hands and Q&A to review the details of the reorganization and changes to how we work. Next week, we'll get together in person as planned to dive deeper on our H2 plans.
For over a decade, Patreon has been growing and evolving to ensure that creators are properly compensated for the value they create in the world. We have successfully adapted and thrived through recessions, the chaos of online media, and multiple market shifts. We continue to come out the other side stronger than before, and that's exactly what we'll do again.

content_html_zh:
Patreon 将解雇 20% 的员工。 阅读首席执行官发给员工的备忘录。

作者：Sydney Bradley 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

Patreon 是一个为内容付费的创作者平台，目前正在裁员。

在一份致 Patreon 内容创作者的公开报告中，首席执行官杰克·康特 (Jack Conte) 宣布，这家创作者经济初创公司将解雇 93 名员工，约占其员工总数的 20%。

孔特在给员工的另一份备忘录中表示，“我们运营的市场在过去 6 个月里发生了深刻的变化”，公司需要削减成本，以“为我们的创作者保持稳定、可靠的基石”。

孔特说，是的，人工智能确实在其中发挥了作用。

孔特在备忘录中表示：“人工智能从根本上改变了科技行业，变革的步伐从未如此强烈，我预计它会变得更快。”

不过，Patreon 强调，这些裁员并不是因为公司认为人工智能可以取代人类或创造力。

今年三月，孔特告诉《商业内幕》，虽然人工智能可以帮助人们创造“美丽的事物”，但公司推出人工智能工具时不应该“以一种只会给世界创意人士带来一场血腥屠杀的方式”。

孔特还发布了有关该主题的视频，建议人工智能公司开始向创作者付费。

Patreon 作为一家公司的精神是基于付费创作者。 它成立于 2013 年，现已成为 YouTuber 和播客等创作者的热门平台，让粉丝为内容和社区付费。

随着创作者经济的复苏，该公司于 2020 年成为独角兽，2021 年估值达到 40 亿美元。 与许多更广泛的科技和媒体行业一样，Patreon 此后也经历了财务逆风。 2022年9月，该公司裁员80人，约占当时员工的17%。

Patreon 在该领域面临着许多新的竞争对手，例如 Substack、Beehiiv、YouTube 自己的会员级别以及创作者制作的白标应用程序。

它并不是创作者经济中唯一一家裁员的公司。 今年 2 月，创作者商务平台 LTK 进行了裁员，Meta、Snapchat 和 LinkedIn 等其他科技公司今年也进行了裁员。

在最近一轮裁员之后，孔特在给员工的备忘录中表示，公司将对其“组织结构和工作方式”进行改变，即通过“扁平化”公司团队。

该公司将继续专注于为创作者提供产品。

孔特在与创作者分享的帖子中表示：“Patreon 的核心业务健康而强劲，我们将在未来几十年成为创作者的基石。” “我们不会改变我们的路线图或优先事项。 Patreon 将继续推出新功能、改进我们的核心体验、媒体和社区产品等。”

阅读 Patreon 发送给其员工的完整备忘录：

嗨，团队，今天早上我有一个痛苦的更新。 我决定将团队规模减少 20%，即 93 人。 公司的所有团队成员都将在接下来的几分钟内收到一封电子邮件，表明您的角色是否受到这一变化的影响。我将在下面解释这一决定的原因和后勤工作，但在此之前，我想承认这对于组成这家公司的善良、雄心勃勃、勤奋工作和使命驱动的员工来说将是多么痛苦。 Patreon 的员工是我们精神的核心，也是我们文化和业务如此特别的原因。 今天对于很多人来说都是艰难的，作为这个决定以及导致这个决定的负责人，我不会掉以轻心。 今天请抽出您需要的时间来支持我们即将离职的队友和您自己。发生了什么变化以及原因我们今天要做两个关键的变化：减少劳动力改变我们的组织结构以及我们的工作方式我将在下面逐一解释。 劳动力的减少我们的核心业务没有任何改变——它仍然强劲且始终如一：世界上几乎每个国家的超过 300,000 名创作者都在我们的平台上赚钱。创作者每年在 Patreon 上赚取数十亿美元，并且这些收入继续呈现稳定、强劲的增长。创作者、会员、收入和处理量每月继续呈现强劲、持续的增长。最重要的是，我们向媒体和社区网络的转型正在开始发挥作用：我们的网络是现在每月向创作者发送 150 万个新会员。在过去 3 年里，创作者增加了约 2 亿免费会员。自我们网络推出以来，Feed 会员数量增加了 5 倍以上。但我们的转型需要时间。 我们正在关注重大问题：注意力经济正在针对成瘾而不是长期关系进行优化。 创作者发现越来越难以接触到他们的追随者并建立强大的社区。 传统的社交平台将创作者锁定在他们的系统中，而不是给予他们独立性。 没有一家公司比 Patreon 更能解决这些问题。与此同时，我们所在的市场在过去 6 个月里发生了深刻的变化。 我们需要调整我们的成本结构，以确保在我们努力实现长期目标的过程中，我们仍然是我们的创作者稳定、可靠的基石。 我们今天所做的改变是痛苦的，但它们是必要的，以确保 Patreon 能够花我们需要的时间从一个强有力的位置解决上述问题。2。 改变我们的组织结构和工作方式我们正在对我们的组织结构和工作方式进行一些改变。 具体来说，我们正在扁平化组织，让团队重新聚焦于我们的首要任务，并发展我们运营的关键方面，使我们能够更快地适应变化。我们之所以做出这些改变，是因为团队规模较小，我们需要一个新的组织结构来继续有效地执行我们不变的战略和优先事项。 我们仍然专注于：改善平台上的核心创作者和粉丝体验，以及帮助创作者通过我们的网络扩大受众和业务此外，我希望我们作为一家公司能够提高敏捷性。 人工智能从根本上改变了科技行业，变化的步伐从未如此强烈，而且我预计它会变得更快。 随着时间的推移，我们的工作性质不断发生变化，我们每个人都将在重新定义我们的工作方式方面发挥作用。人工智能并不能取代人类创造力。过去几个月讨论最多的问题之一是人工智能是否正在取代人类。要明确人工智能对今天决策的影响：我们不会做出上述改变，因为我们相信人工智能会取代人类。我们学会使用这些新工具越多，就越清楚它们并不能取代创造力、判断力、细节导向或创造力。我们的队友所拥有的精湛技艺，也不能取代我们所有人都如此珍视的对人际关系的渴望。 这是我个人的观点，但更重要的是，这是 Patreon 战略的基础：我们的产品愿景和业务都以人类创造力和人际联系的价值为基础。不过，人工智能从根本上改变了科技行业，包括我们的工作方式、我们如何构建产品、我们如何沟通等等。 这确实对我们的运营和组织方式产生了影响。今日物流致即将离开的团队成员：在您从我们的人员团队收到的电子邮件中，您将找到有关后续步骤、遣散费的详细信息，以及有关如何在有任何问题时与人员团队成员联系的信息。 您还会收到来自我们高级领导团队成员的单独消息，邀请您参加个人通话（如果您愿意）。您可以在太平洋时间今天下午 5 点之前访问您的 Patreon 笔记本电脑和 Slack。 对所有其他内部系统的访问将被删除，因为我们希望确保保护我们的创作者及其业务。我知道人们能够说再见是多么重要，因此我们创建了一个 #gratitude-and-farewell Slack 频道，作为我们所有人联系的地方，如果您愿意的话。遣散费详细信息：我们已经准备了遣散费，以支持受影响的员工度过这一过渡：从今天开始 16 周的工资，包括在我们的 8 月份仍保留在工资单上第 20 个归属日。在公司工作的每满一年可额外获得一周的工资。额外现金支付 a) 尚未达到 1 年悬崖的新员工，以及 b) 尚未收到 2026 年更新补助金的长期队友。符合条件的员工和家庭将享受到年底的医疗保险。1,500 美元的津贴用于更换公司笔记本电脑。感谢您为我们的创作者、粉丝和这家公司。 你们的贡献给很多人的生活带来了巨大的变化，我对你们在工作中投入的精力、技巧和关怀深表感谢。 这项工作现在已成为 Patreon 的一部分，并将在未来几年继续造福创作者及其社区。 对于我们剩下的队友：今天的焦点将是我们即将离开的队友，但我们已经安排了一个简短的视频通话，让我们留下来作为一个团队在一起。 明天，我们将举行全体会议并进行问答，以审查重组的细节以及我们工作方式的变化。 下周，我们将按计划亲自聚在一起，更深入地讨论我们的 H2 计划。十多年来，Patreon 一直在成长和发展，以确保创作者为他们在世界上创造的价值获得适当的补偿。 我们成功地适应了经济衰退、网络媒体的混乱和多重市场转变，并蓬勃发展。 我们继续让对方变得比以前更强大，这正是我们将再次做的。

有小费吗？ 您还可以通过加密消息应用程序 Signal (@sydneykbradley.123)、加密电子邮件 (sydneykbradley@proton.me) 或标准电子邮件 (sbradley@businessinsider.com) 与她联系。 使用个人电子邮件地址、非工作 WiFi 网络和非工作设备；这是我们安全共享信息的指南。
